title: gouldia terminalis var. arborescens
intro: Gouldia terminalis var. arborescens, also known as the tree form of the Hawaiian gouldia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae that is endemic to Hawaii. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Gouldia terminalis var. arborescens include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
